## The Code: Transformer Block in Python

```{.python include="src/python/common.py" snippet="layer_norm_vec"}
```

Layer normalization shifts a single vector to mean 0, variance 1, then applies learned scale and shift.
`LayerNorm` holds the learned parameters $\gamma$ and $\beta$.
`make_layer_norm` initializes them to identity.

```{.python include="src/python/common.py" snippet="layer_norm_matrix"}
```

`layer_norm_matrix` applies `layer_norm_vector` independently to each row of a $[T \times d]$ matrix.

```{.python include="src/python/common.py" snippet="transformer_block_params"}
```

`TransformerBlock` groups the attention module, feed-forward module, and two layer norms.
`make_transformer_block` allocates their parameters.

```{.python include="src/python/common.py" snippet="transformer_block_forward"}
```

`transformer_block` is the Pre-LN residual block: normalize, attend, add the residual, normalize, apply FFN, add the residual again.
`forward_stack` threads the sequence through $N$ blocks.

```{.python include="src/python/chapter_demos.py" snippet="chapter_10"}
```

The demo runs one transformer block over a three-token sequence and checks that layer norm and the block both preserve the outer shape.
Run it with `python3 src/python/chapter_demos.py`.
